rulings.Case Title: LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC VS. MARTIN LEE ENG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Legal Recovery, Llc???S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determining Validity Of Third Party Claims (Motions)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC PLAINTIFF LEGAL RECOVERY, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO DETERMINING VALIDITY OF THIRD PARTY CLAIMS (MOTIONS) is GRANTED. Based on the procedures set forth in CCP 720.110 moving party filed its moving papers within 15 days of the filing of the third-party claims and set a hearing within 20 days of filing. Amended Memorandum and Declaration is stricken as non-complaint with CCP 720.310(a). Based on the moving papers filed on November 22, 2023 and opposition filed by Martin Eng only on December 4, 2023, the Court finds that the claims subject to November 22, 2023 motion are not valid. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LSC 906-908 BROADWAY LLC VS. STARTUP TEMPLE HOLDINGS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Judgment To Set The Deficiency Judgment Against Guarantor Pavel Cherkashin, Only; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities And Declaration Of Eugene Rozovsky In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2023 line 4. PLAINTIFF LSC 906-908 BROADWAY LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO AMEND JUDGMENT TO SET THE DEFICIENCY JUDGMENT AGAINST GUARANTOR PAVEL CHERKASHIN, ONLY; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES AND DECLARATION OF EUGENE ROZOVSKY IN SUPPORT THEREOF is OFF CALENDAR. Moving party failed to provide a complete courtesy copy of its motion. While the courtesy copy contains no tabs, it is obvious that exhibits 3-7 are missing as the last page of the documents provided bears the following language in the footer "Joseph J. Blake and Associates, Inc, Real Estate Valuation and Consulting." rulings.Case Title: ROSA MARTINEZ VS. AIRBNB, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2023 line 5. DEFENDANT AIRBNB, INC., AIRBNB PAYMENTS, LLC NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION AND STAY PROCEEDINGS is DENIED. Moving party failed to meet its burden demonstrating the existence of an arbitration agreement between Plaintiff and Defendant by a preponderance of the evidence. Plaintiff is not bound by the arbitration agreement between Decedent and Defendant. (Monschke v. Timber Ridge Assisted Living (2016) 244 Cal.App.4th 583.) Plaintiff's unauthorized sur-reply is stricken and was not considered by the Court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: 819 ELLIS CF HOLDINGS, LLC VS. PHILIP ACHILLES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication.
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for December 11, 2023 line 14. PLAINTIFF 819 ELLIS CF HOLDINGS, LLC MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. The Motion for Summary Judgement is DENIED. Plaintiff fails to meet its burden to establish evidence that Defendant Achilles, Fay, and A.A. Parking, Inc. are still in possession of the premises for a cause of action based on holdover possession by a previous owner. CCP 1161a(b)(2.) Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. (Evid. Code, 452(h) and 453.) The Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The motion is denied as to issues 1 and 2: Plaintiff fails to meet its burden to establish evidence that Defendant Achilles, Fay, and A.A. Parking, Inc. are still in possession of the premises for a cause of action based on holdover possession by a previous owner. CCP 1161a(b)(2.) The motion is GRANTED as to the affirmative defenses, subject to opposition. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
